validate.description: Diff customized harness and env files against upstream scaffold to catch drift. Use when checking fullsend config, comparing harness files, or verifying customizations before deploying.

inspect.description: Investigate a fullsend agent run — pull together status, timing, agent output, and logs. Use when debugging a failed run, checking run results, or looking at a specific run by ID or issue number.

trigger.description: Post a fullsend slash command on a GitHub issue or PR to start an agent run. Use when triggering /fs-code, /fs-triage, /fs-review, /fs-fix, /fs-retro, /fs-prioritize, or /fs-debug.

debug.description: Run sandbox environment diagnostics — checks env vars, corepack, yarn, proxy, network, filesystem, and GCP credentials. Posts results as a comment on the issue.

watch.description: Monitor a triggered fullsend run until completion, then auto-inspect it. Use when waiting for a fullsend agent run to finish.

comment.description: Post a comment on a GitHub issue or PR. Use when adding context, instructions, or notes before or after a fullsend agent run.

label.description: Add or remove a label on a GitHub issue or PR. Use when managing fullsend labels like triaged, ready-to-code, fullsend-no-fix, or workspace labels.

upgrade.description: Upgrade Fullsend to a target release by updating the CLI, syncing scaffold-managed files, and checking customized files for drift. Use when bumping Fullsend or checking upgrade impact.

help.description: Explain the Fullsend agent pipeline, available agents, local deployment, and common onboarding workflows. Use when learning how Fullsend works or getting started.

custom-agents.description: Guide the creation of a custom standalone Fullsend agent, including scaffold, dispatch, harness, policy, and security wiring. Use when building or extending Fullsend agents.
